description:
Your working directory contains an API-test workspace at
    /home/user/api_tests.

    1. Inside that workspace there is an existing tree called
       /home/user/api_tests/logs/ … with many sub-folders and dozens
       of *.log files gathered by the CI pipeline.

    2. A QA analyst now wants a single text file that lists every line
       in every *.log file that begins with the exact string

            FAIL:

       The analyst wants:

       • One result per line.  
       • The line must have three colon-separated fields produced in
         the same way GNU grep’s “-n” option prints them:

              RELATIVE_PATH_TO_LOG:LINE_NUMBER:FAIL: …full line text…

         Example (spacing, punctuation, and case matter):

              logs/serviceX/2024-06-06.log:3:FAIL: /ping returned 500

       • “RELATIVE_PATH_TO_LOG” must be **relative to the workspace
         root** (/home/user/api_tests), not an absolute path.  
       • The results must be sorted in ordinary byte-wise
         (LC_ALL=C) order so that the automated checker has a
         deterministic ordering.

    3. Save the collected, sorted list to

           /home/user/api_tests/reports/failure_summary.txt

       The “reports” directory is expected by other tooling; create it
       first if it does not already exist.

    4. You may accomplish the whole job with a *single* compound shell
       pipeline that combines find and xargs (plus any helper commands
       you need, e.g. grep, sort …).  No intermediate or temporary
       files are allowed; the final artefact must be the single file
       mentioned above.

    5. When you are finished, the only user-visible deliverable must be
       the text file at the exact path given.  Its content must follow
       the three-field “grep -n” layout **precisely**, with no leading
       or trailing blank lines.

    Automated tests will verify:

       • The file exists at the exact path.  
       • It is world-readable.  
       • Its byte content matches the required sorted list *exactly*
         (checksum comparison).

    You do not need to display the file on screen after creation (the
    checker will open it), but feel free to print a confirmation
    message if you like.

    Reminder: do not hard-code absolute paths inside the result file;
    they must be relative, starting with “logs/…”.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data for automated verification.
        The harness will create these four log files *before* the
        agent runs and will set their modification times so that Find
        sees them.

        1. /home/user/api_tests/logs/serviceA/2024-06-08.log
           1  OK: ping 200
           2  FAIL: /users endpoint returned 500
           3  OK: /orders endpoint returned 201

        2. /home/user/api_tests/logs/serviceB/2024-06-08.log
           1  FAIL: token refresh failed
           2  FAIL: login API timeout
           3  OK: healthcheck 200

        3. /home/user/api_tests/logs/serviceC/2024-06-07.log
           1  OK: initial handshake 200
           2  FAIL: search API 502
           3  OK: teardown 200

        4. /home/user/api_tests/logs/serviceC/archive/2024-05-30.log
           1  FAIL: should not be included because old
           2  OK: cleanup complete

        The test expects the final artefact to be the file
        /home/user/api_tests/reports/failure_summary.txt
        containing *exactly* the five lines below, in this order:

        logs/serviceA/2024-06-08.log:2:FAIL: /users endpoint returned 500
        logs/serviceB/2024-06-08.log:1:FAIL: token refresh failed
        logs/serviceB/2024-06-08.log:2:FAIL: login API timeout
        logs/serviceC/2024-06-07.log:2:FAIL: search API 502
        logs/serviceC/archive/2024-05-30.log:1:FAIL: should not be included because old

        No extra whitespace or newline outside those five lines is
        permitted.  The file must be world-readable (mode 0644).
    -->
name: task_000000_17f5cd1a
